premise: In the neon-lit waterways beneath the city, underground surfers design and build their own hydro-boards to ride the storm drains. Across one season, two rivals — Circuit, a gadget-obsessed monkey engineer, and Riptide, a fearless cat daredevil — trade wins, defeats, and new strategies in a season-long duel that builds toward the legendary Sewer Crown race.
style_anchor: clean, simple 3D cartoon style like the modern Super Mario Bros. Movie and Nintendo/Illumination games: smooth simplified surfaces, glossy toy-like vinyl shading, bold simple shapes, strongly exaggerated NON-realistic proportions — a big oversized rounded head about one-third of total body height, very large round expressive eyes taking up much of the face, small rounded body, short stubby limbs, simplified appealing features. Minimal surface texture — fur and skin read as smooth, simple, flat-shaded cartoon forms, NOT individual hair strands, pores, or realistic detail. Bright saturated candy colors, soft clean cinematic lighting with gentle ambient occlusion and glossy wet highlights, shallow depth of field. Deliberately non-realistic, polished video-game-cartoon look — never photorealistic, never realistic fur, skin, or anatomy. Clothes are not detailed in terms of design, rely on silhouette

characters.name: circuit
characters.appearance_tokens: a small wiry monkey with warm golden-brown fur, an oversized rounded head with huge curious amber eyes, and a long prehensile tail; brainy gadgeteer in cool-toned beach streetwear — an oversized cyan-and-white mesh-panel windbreaker worn open, a baggy graphic tee, roomy cargo board-shorts, and chunky neon-yellow pool slides; a snorkel-and-goggle set pushed up on his head and a clear waterproof gadget sling-bag, signature yellow number 3; rides a sensor-studded hydro-surfboard bristling with small fins
characters.personality: brainy, calculating, over-prepared; wins by out-engineering the course with gadgets, telemetry, and clever lines

characters.name: riptide
characters.appearance_tokens: a lean, athletic cat with sleek charcoal-black fur, an oversized head with huge sharp green eyes, a torn ear and a spiky fur mohawk; fearless daredevil in bold warm-toned beach streetwear — a fitted sleeveless magenta-and-black graphic rashguard tank under an open palm-print bomber, long flame-and-wave print board-shorts, a shark-tooth cord necklace and grippy neon-orange reef shoes, signature white number 7; rides a stripped-down minimalist neon-orange hydro-surfboard built for raw speed
characters.personality: reckless, fearless, rides on pure instinct and adrenaline; takes insane lines no sane surfer would attempt

active_threads: Circuit and Riptide are the scene's two top riders; the season-long duel for the Sewer Crown is just beginning
plot_state.last_cliffhanger: Dead even on day one — but the Crown only fits one head. Whose board breaks first?

world_anchor: Riders ride custom finned hydro-surfboards, standing or crouched, surfing fast-rushing water through neon storm drains and flood tunnels. The vehicles are always water surfboards — never motorcycles, bikes, wheels, handlebars, rails, train tracks, or cars; the surface is always rushing water, never roads or tracks.
logline: Two rival riders build custom hydro-boards and battle across a season for the Sewer Crown.
setting: A vast underground world of storm drains, flood tunnels, and sluice gates beneath a rain-soaked city. Rushing water, glowing algae, graffiti-tagged concrete, and shafts of streetlight through the grates above.
episode_format: Each episode is a ~60-75s self-contained beat that ALSO advances the season arc. Always: a frame-1 hook, a clear stakes setup, the Circuit-vs-Riptide contrast (gadgets vs. raw nerve) where relevant, and a loopable cliffhanger that pulls toward the next episode and the looming Sewer Crown. NOT every episode is a race — vary the purpose per `arc.episode_purposes` (a new strategy, a defeat, a character or relationship beat, a training run, rising buildup) so races feel like special, anticipated events.
tone: high-energy Pixar-style sports rivalry with comedic beats and real emotional stakes

arc.synopsis: A 20-episode season-long rivalry between Circuit and Riptide for the Sewer Crown. Episode 1 throws viewers straight into a high-stakes race. From there the season is rising action — alternating wins and defeats, new boards and strategies, qualifying heats, and deepening rivalry-into-respect — escalating tension toward the climactic Sewer Crown race.
arc.finale: Episode 20 IS the Sewer Crown race: the season's biggest event, it reveals the winner and closes on a victory lap and the resolution of the Circuit-Riptide relationship.
episode_purposes: a high-stakes race or qualifying heat (a special, spaced-out tentpole event)
inventing, building, or testing a new board, gadget, or strategy
a defeat or setback that forces a rethink
a character-development or backstory beat (why they ride)
deepening, testing, or fracturing the Circuit-Riptide relationship
a training montage or risky trial run
a rival crew or external threat to the scene
rising-tension buildup and stakes-raising toward an upcoming race
arc.pacing_notes: Open the season (ep 1) on a high-stakes race to hook viewers. After that, DON'T make every episode a race — space races out as anticipated tentpole events and fill between them with strategy, character, and relationship episodes. Every episode plants or pays off a seed for the Sewer Crown finale. As the episode number approaches 20, raise the stakes; episode 20 delivers the Crown race, the winner, and a victory lap.
